blocks.body: Comparative (highest degree) is used to compare two subjects, and Superlative (highest degree) is used to single out one of the group. Correct understanding of this difference is key to natural English speaking.

{ "title": "9. Short summary", "items": [ "Comparative — for comparing two objects: bigger than, more interesting than.", "Superlative — to highlight one of the group: the biggest, the most interesting.", "The Than marker almost always indicates the need for a comparative.", "The marker the indicates a superlative before an adjective.", "Prepositions in/of — after a superlative show a group or place.", "Special case — \"of the two\" can go with the superlative: the taller of the two.", "Avoid mistakes - don't use the with the comparative, don't skip the the with the superlative." ] }
